The way we do business is shifting, and in the coming months and years, entrepreneurs will have to sell their services and products to an AI agent before they ever reach a human.

Buyers are increasingly relying on AI tools to evaluate their options and run comparative product analyses. Therefore, ensuring your business appears favorably in these AI-generated comparisons is becoming a major strategic priority.

We are already seeing this shift with updates like Meta’s Andromeda, where the AI actively goes out to find the specific people it thinks are most likely to buy your offer. If you aren’t visible when an agent conducts its research, you will miss out on the sale.

Fortunately, you can use AI deep research right now to understand how these agents view your industry and your positioning. 

Try running a full competitive analysis with an AI tool to see how your offer stacks up, which can help you discover opportunities to expand or future-proof your business.

Want to learn exactly how to set up these deep research workflows? 

🎧 Watch or listen to the latest episode of AI Explored podcast with @stelzner and guest expert @nataliemacneil.

Have you tested how your brand shows up in an AI agent’s research yet? Share your thoughts, concerns, and ideas in the comments! 

👋 -Jason

Have you tried re-ordering your carousel slides yet? 
(That’s my favorite update!)

Here are several Instagram updates we’re keeping our eyes on right now:

1️⃣ DM shares are now a primary ranking signal. Content that sparks a private conversation will always out-reach content that only gets a “Like.” 

2️⃣ Automated “AI Modified” labels are live. Data shows that “unpolished” authentic content still holds a slight retention edge. 

3️⃣ You can no longer test duplicate files. Use Trial Reels to test unique creative hooks before they hit your main grid. 

4️⃣ Native Checkout: Native Stripe and PayPal integrations mean lower friction and higher conversion rates for IG Shops. 

5️⃣ …and of course, you can now re-order slides after posting. 

👇 Which of these updates will change your workflow the most going forward? 

👋 -Jason

#SMMW26

The “Link in Bio” just got some competition.
(At least that’s what could be coming to an Instagram account near you!)

For over a decade, Instagram has protected the “sanctity” of the feed by blocking outbound links in captions. That’s changing.

Meta is officially testing clickable caption links, but it isn’t a free-for-all. 

To keep the “spam” at bay, it’s being tested with these parameters: 
✅ Meta Verified users only 
✅ 10 links per month max 
✅ Creator/Personal accounts (for now)

Is this a shift in how we think about Instagram traffic?

I guess time will tell! 🧐
👋 -Jason
